nodes.name: CLAUDE.md
nodes.summary: Main project rules and instruction file for Claude AI assistant - defines behavioral guidelines, workflow profiles (minimal/standard/full/audit), checkpoint mechanism, skill execution contracts, tool selection principles, and degradation detection rules

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Project documentation - describes three-layer agent orchestration architecture (Kimi plans, DeepSeek builds, MiniMax queries), API endpoints, plugin tools, environment configuration, and health check procedures

nodes.name: opencode.json
nodes.summary: OpenCode plugin configuration - enables 4 agent tools (agent, agent_status, agent_checkpoint, agent_execute_skills) and defines workflow profiles with phase counts

nodes.name: package.json
nodes.summary: Node.js package manifest - defines agent-orchestrator v1.0.0 with main entry index.js, depends on @opencode-ai/plugin and zod, uses Bun runtime

nodes.name: Workflow Profile System
nodes.summary: Four workflow profiles (minimal 5 steps, standard 37 steps, full 69 steps, audit 69 steps + report) that control execution depth of the orchestration workflow

nodes.name: Three-Layer Agent Orchestration
nodes.summary: Core architecture pattern where Kimi K2.6 plans tasks, DeepSeek V4 Flash executes code, and MiniMax M3 handles queries - coordinated through a Bun+SQLite server

nodes.name: Checkpoint Mechanism
nodes.summary: 17 checkpoints (7 INTERACTIVE + 10 TOOL_REQUIRED) that pause workflow for user verification, with freshness validation to ensure artifacts are generated in current session

nodes.name: Skill-Driven Execution Contract
nodes.summary: P0/P1/P2 skill hierarchy - 5 mandatory P0 skills (brainstorming, writing-plans, TDD, verification, branch-finishing), 10 P1 skills, 16 P2 GStack skills

nodes.name: Plugin Tools
nodes.summary: 4 OpenCode plugin tools: agent (auto-router), agent_execute_skills (skill parser+dispatcher), agent_status (health monitor), agent_checkpoint (milestone verifier)

nodes.name: Degradation Detection
nodes.summary: Rules preventing workflow degradation - prohibits using stale graphs, skipping failed tools, partial skill loading, or premature conclusions

nodes.name: ARCHITECTURE.md
nodes.summary: Architecture documentation for the agent orchestrator three-layer multi-model system. Defines Kimi K2.6 (strategic planning), DeepSeek V4 Flash (tactical code execution), and MiniMax M3 (operational read-only queries). Covers SQLite schema with 6 tables, checkpoint lifecycle, REST/WebSocket communication protocols, routing logic, error handling, security, and scaling considerations.

nodes.name: CHECKPOINT-SYSTEM.md
nodes.summary: Checkpoint mechanism documentation for the full workflow. Defines 17 checkpoints (7 INTERACTIVE + 10 TOOL_REQUIRED) that pause workflow execution for user input. Covers command checkpoints (/understand), interactive skill checkpoints (brainstorming, executing-plans, etc.), tool skill checkpoints (TDD, debugging, QA, etc.), freshness verification via file mtime, and execution flow across 4 phases.

nodes.name: CODEGRAPH-CAPABILITY-MATRIX.md
nodes.summary: CodeGraph capability matrix documenting 16 CLI commands and 5 MCP tools. CLI commands include init, uninit, index, sync, status, query, files, context, serve, unlock, callers, callees, impact, affected, install, uninstall. MCP tools include context, search, node, explore, trace. Documents CLI-only commands (11), MCP-only capabilities (3), and fallback strategies.

nodes.name: WORKFLOW-PROFILES.md
nodes.summary: Workflow profile system documentation. Defines 4 profiles: minimal (5 steps, 30s for daily commit checks), standard (37 steps, 3-5min for major features), full (69 steps, 10-20min for releases), audit (69 steps + audit report, 15-30min). Each profile controls which phases execute and how many steps run.

nodes.name: agent-orchestrator-analysis-2026-06-04.md
nodes.summary: Complete workflow analysis report for Agent Orchestrator dated 2026-06-04. Documents 4-phase analysis: knowledge graph construction (27,538 nodes, 38,538 edges), principle loading (Karpathy's 10 coding principles), deep analysis (plugin tools, system.transform hook, CAPABILITY_LIST), and audit verification (154 tests passing). Identifies 9 core components and provides recommendations.
sections: Phase 2: 原则与工作流加载

nodes.name: codegraph-capability-matrix.sh
nodes.summary: Generates capability matrix for CodeGraph tools by extracting CLI commands from `codegraph --help` and comparing with MCP tools list. Outputs JSON to docs/CODEGRAPH-CAPABILITY-MATRIX.json with CLI vs MCP overlap analysis.

nodes.name: skills-inventory.sh
nodes.summary: Health check for 31 skills (Karpathy 1 + Superpowers 14 + GStack 16). Scans skill directories and reports status, size, mtime for each. Exit code 0=all OK, 1=has missing skills.

nodes.name: verify-skills-execution.sh
nodes.summary: Verifies P0 skill execution with 8 checks across 2 phases: tool execution results (CodeGraph context/impact, Graphify query/path, /understand knowledge graph) and skill execution results (brainstorming specs, writing-plans, TDD commits, verification, finishing-branch, debug reports, subagent dispatch). Supports --fresh mode for workflow-start freshness validation. Outputs .gstack/skill-execution-log.md.

nodes.name: workflow-dry-run.sh
nodes.summary: Workflow dry-run with checkpoint mechanism. Validates workflow executability without modifying files or calling LLM. Supports 4 profiles (minimal/standard/full/audit). Implements 17 checkpoints (7 INTERACTIVE + 10 TOOL_REQUIRED) with product freshness validation. Exit codes: 0=pass, 1=P0 blocked, 2=warning, 3=tool missing.

nodes.name: workflow-preflight-check.sh
nodes.summary: Pre-flight check for complete workflow (11 checks). Validates: CodeGraph CLI, Understand-Anything plugin + build status, Graphify CLI + Python deps, GStack-OpenCode, Superpowers, CodeGraph index, graph freshness (24h), /understand knowledge graph, CodeGraph CLI 16 commands, Understand 7 SKILL.md files, 31 skills inventory.

layers.name: Plugin Entry Layer
layers.description: OpenCode plugin entry point and package configuration. index.js is the AgentOrchestratorPlugin that wires together all subsystems and exposes tools to the OpenCode runtime.

layers.name: API Layer
layers.description: REST API endpoints exposed by the HTTP server. Each file defines Express routes for a specific domain (plans, checkpoints, status, threads, internal events).

layers.name: Core Orchestration Layer
layers.description: Central orchestration logic. Auto-dispatcher routes tasks to appropriate handlers. Auto-executor builds skill execution prompts. Plan-orchestrator generates and manages plans. Milestone-manager tracks checkpoint progress. Subagent-runner dispatches work to model-backed subagents.

layers.name: Model Layer
layers.description: LLM model client implementations. Kimi handles planning (Plan Mode), DeepSeek handles execution (Build Mode), MiniMax provides fallback/specialized capabilities. All clients share a common interface for the orchestration layer.

layers.name: Infrastructure Layer
layers.description: Shared infrastructure services. SQLite database layer (db.js) handles persistence and schema. Event system (events.js) provides pub/sub messaging. WebSocket server enables real-time communication with OpenCode frontend.

layers.name: Documentation Layer
layers.description: Project documentation including architecture docs, checkpoint system docs, AI assistant instructions (CLAUDE.md), and project README.

tour.title: Project Overview — Documentation & Configuration
tour.description: Start here to understand what this project does and how it's configured. README.md describes the three-layer architecture (Kimi → DeepSeek → MiniMax), the API endpoints, and plugin tools. CLAUDE.md contains the AI assistant's behavioral rules and project-specific constraints. opencode.json registers the plugin with OpenCode, and package.json defines dependencies and scripts.

tour.title: Plugin Entry Point — index.js
tour.description: The heart of the system. AgentOrchestratorPlugin is an async function that OpenCode loads as a plugin. It initializes the SQLite database, creates the three model clients (Kimi, DeepSeek, MiniMax), starts the AutoDispatcher, and registers four tools: `agent` (auto-route requests), `agent_status` (system health), `agent_checkpoint` (milestone reviews), and `agent_execute_skills` (skill dispatch). The `agent` tool is the main entry — it calls `executePlanTask()` which asks Kimi to decide between 'plan mode' (analysis only) or 'build mode' (generate plan + execute items).

tour.title: HTTP Server — server/index.js
tour.description: A standalone Bun HTTP server (port 8765) that exposes REST API endpoints and serves a real-time dashboard. It initializes the same SQLite database, sets up WebSocket for live event broadcasting, and routes requests to the API modules. This server runs independently from the plugin — the plugin registers tools with OpenCode, while the server provides external HTTP access for monitoring and direct API calls.

tour.title: API Layer — REST Endpoints
tour.description: Five API modules handle HTTP requests: plan-api.js (create/retrieve/update plans), checkpoint-api.js (create/verify milestone checkpoints), thread-api.js (conversation thread management), status-api.js (system health and agent availability), and internal-event-api.js (internal event handling for inter-component communication). Each router reads from and writes to the shared SQLite database.

tour.title: Core Orchestration — Planning & Dispatching
tour.description: Three modules form the orchestration brain. PlanOrchestrator generates structured plans via Kimi (with DeepSeek fallback), validates them with PlanParser, and persists to the database. AutoDispatcher routes subagent tasks through two paths: D1 (direct LLM API via SubagentRunner) or D2 (long-lived opencode server process). AutoExecutor builds execution prompts for skill-based workflows, categorizing skills as INTERACTIVE (user input needed), TOOL_REQUIRED (needs bash/browse), or AUTO (can run headlessly in a subagent).

tour.title: Model Clients — Three-Layer LLM Architecture
tour.description: Each model has a dedicated client extending BaseModelClient. KimiClient (Moonshot K2.6) handles planning: task mode analysis, plan generation, checkpoint review, and recommendation generation — it's the 'brain' that decides what to do. DeepSeekClient (V4 Flash) handles execution: code generation, task execution, and serves as Kimi's fallback. MiniMaxClient (M3) handles queries: code search and verification tasks. The `chatWithFallback()` pattern ensures resilience — if Kimi fails, DeepSeek takes over plan generation.

tour.title: Infrastructure — Database, Events & WebSocket
tour.description: The persistence and communication layer. DB (db.js) wraps Bun's SQLite with methods for plans, plan items, checkpoints, activity logs, and threads. The schema (db-schema.js) defines 7 tables. Events (events.js) provides typed event emission for plan lifecycle (created/completed), item progress (started/completed), checkpoint creation/verification, and model fallback notifications. The WebSocket server broadcasts these events in real-time to the dashboard and any connected clients.

tour.title: Checkpoint System — Milestone Quality Gates
tour.description: The quality assurance mechanism. MilestoneManager tracks plan progress and triggers checkpoints every N completed items (default: 4). When a checkpoint is created, it collects all agent outputs up to the milestone index. Kimi then reviews the checkpoint — verifying that work quality meets standards before allowing continuation. If Kimi is unavailable, checkpoints auto-pass with a warning. The checkpoint API (checkpoint-api.js) exposes this for external access, while the `agent_checkpoint` tool provides it to OpenCode.

project.name: agent-orchestrator
project.description: A multi-agent orchestration system for OpenCode that coordinates planning, execution, and verification through specialized agents
